title: Hail to the FLP bar topnotchers and passers
primary_topics: FLP scholars' performance in the 2025 bar examinations
FLP's merit-based Legal Scholarship Program with Tan Yan Kee Foundation
ethical practice and disbarment as the title's revocability
sub_topics: 14 FLP scholars topped or passed the 2025 bar exams
Three FLP scholars in the top 20: Enrico Gabriel Paguia (ADMU, 6th), Ignacio Lorenzo Villareal (UP, 15th), Chinzen Viernes (UP, 19th)

11 other passers: Mariana Alberto (UP), Joanna Rizza David (Bulacan State), Kerstein Kylle Despi (USC), Susanna Ruth Gruyal (UP), Cicily Isabelle Mirasol (UP), Christa Marie Mutuc (USC), Jayson Orajay (Xavier), Allan Cairo Reyes (UP), Janelle Sy (ADMU), Ricardo Anton SobreviÃ±as (ADMU), Mary Lynn Te (USC)

FLP's historical track record: 2018 (Borja 1st ADMU, Gaw 5th ADMU, Dy 17th UP)

2025 bar examiners praised: retired SAJ Estela M. Perlas-Bernabe (civil law), Judge Cherry Chiara L. Hernando (commercial and taxation), SBN Justice Sarah Jane T. Fernandez (criminal law), retired CA Justice Magdangal M. De Leon (labor)
Justice Amy C. Lazaro-Javier, overall chair of the 2025 bar exams

notable_anecdotes.summary: FLP's first 2018 batch of 10 examinees led by Sean James Borja (ADMU, 1st place), Katrina Monica Gaw (5th), and Ervin Fredrick Dy (17th)

2024: Kyle Christian Tutor (UP, 1st), Maria Lovelyn Joyce Quebrar (UP, 6th), Betlee-kyle Barraquias (ADMU, 18th)

During the 2020-2021 pandemic SC did not rank top 20, opting for 'exemplary' (85-90%) and 'excellent' (>90%) categories â€” FLP's Stephanie Mae Domingo, Geremae Mata, and Jani Omamalin all received excellent

CJ praises retired SAJ Estela M. Perlas-Bernabe (FLP trustee) as 2025 civil law examiner alongside personal friends Judge Hernando (commercial/taxation), SBN Justice Fernandez (criminal), and retired CA Justice De Leon (labor)

CJ singles out 'the loudest clap' for Justice Amy C. Lazaro-Javier, overall 2025 bar exam chair, especially for authoring Pimentel v. House (Dec. 3, 2025), which held that PhilHealth contributions belong to its contributors and cannot fund 'wantonly fraudulent public works projects'
